ogTitle: Hornsdale vs Horsnell Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hornsdale and Horsnell Gully.
heading: Hornsdale vs Horsnell Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hornsdale, South Australia and Horsnell Gully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Horsnell Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hornsdale (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hornsdale or Horsnell Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hornsdale has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Horsnell Gully scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hornsdale scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Horsnell Gully scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hornsdale or Horsnell Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Horsnell Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
